findings.summary: Case reports describe rising INR and bleeding events in patients stabilized on warfarin shortly after they began taking guggul-containing lipid-lowering supplements, consistent with reduced warfarin clearance.
findings.citation: Yang et al., J Clin Pharm Ther 2012

findings.summary: In vitro enzyme assays show guggulsterones inhibit CYP2C9-mediated metabolism of model substrates, supporting a pharmacokinetic basis for increased anticoagulant exposure when guggul is co-administered with warfarin.
findings.citation: Brobst et al., Drug Metab Dispos 2004

findings.summary: Traditional Ayurvedic pharmacology texts describe guggulu as having a 'heating' (ushna) potency and caution against combining it with therapies intended to reduce bleeding tendency, an early empirical echo of the modern interaction concern.
